results.pass_condition: Policy requires the district to identify and inventory every automated decision-making technology it uses that generates predictions, recommendations, classifications, rankings, or scores used to make, guide, or assist a decision about an individual.

evidence.heading: DISCIPLINE AND SUPPORTS
evidence.text: No mention of automated monitoring systems, artificial intelligence, predictive scoring tools, or technology-based surveillance mechanisms for identifying bullying behavior was found in this policy. "Cyberbullying" is addressed only as a mode/medium of bullying (electronic communication), not as a technological detection or monitoring system.

results.pass_semantics: Built to the standard we expect the AG to set; subject to final rules.
results.pass_condition: Policy provides a method to determine which systems are 'covered' — i.e., materially influence a consequential decision (a non-de-minimis factor affecting the outcome).

evidence.heading: IMPLEMENTATION CONSIDERATIONS
evidence.text: Ongoing evaluation: periodic assessment of the results of a consolidation, and consideration of any other material factors relevant to a given situation.

results.revision: For each system, the District shall determine whether it materially influences a consequential decision, and if so treat it as a covered system under this policy.

results.pass_semantics: Defensible reading taken where commenters/AG framing disagree; flagged for reconciliation.
results.pass_condition: Policy scopes which education decisions are consequential (enrollment or an education opportunity) and distinguishes routine academic administration and classroom personalization that are excluded.
results.revision: Consequential decisions in education include enrollment and education opportunities such as placement, eligibility, and program access; routine academic administration and classroom personalization that do not materially influence such decisions are excluded.

results.pass_semantics: Built to the standard we expect the AG to set; subject to final rules.
results.pass_condition: Policy flags predictive screeners (including SB 25-200 / READ Act dyslexia screeners) as candidate covered systems pending a materiality analysis.

evidence.heading: AUTOMATED DECISION-MAKING / AI
evidence.text: No automated decision-making, algorithms, AI, machine learning, scoring systems, or predictive tools are mentioned in this policy. The document focuses entirely on data handling, security protocols, and personnel access controls rather than employment screening methodologies.

results.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
results.pass_condition: Policy requires the inventory to be maintained as a current register and re-reviewed on developer material updates.

evidence.heading: AUTHORITY AND RESPONSIBILITIES
evidence.text: Executive Director of Finance: holds overall responsibility for District investment decisions and transactions, including wire and electronic fund transfers. This individual also serves as Board Treasurer and shall consult independent investment advisors as needed. Authorized Personnel: beyond the executive director of finance, the following employees may make investment decisions and conduct transactions: controller; primary accountant (designated in writing by executive director); backup accountant (designated in writing by executive director). Primary and backup accountants must be reported to the Board at the beginning of the fiscal year and whenever changed. Broker/Dealer Standards: authorized broker/dealers must maintain a minimum net capital of $10,000,000 and a ten-year history of services to governmental entities. The executive director and investment advisors maintain lists of eligible brokers with current financial statements.

results.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.

evidence.heading: PROTECTED CLASSES
evidence.text: The categories listed above apply, plus genetic information for employees/applicants.

results.revision: For each covered system, the District shall classify the decision type and the covered domain it affects.

evidence.heading: CONFIDENTIALITY
evidence.text: Information about student and employee communicable disease status must comply with FERPA, HIPAA, the Colorado Open Records Act, and applicable laws. Such information should "only be shared with those persons who have a need to know based upon their legitimate educational, supervisory and/or healthcare responsibilities." Confidentiality protections extend after individuals leave the district.

evidence.heading: GRIEVANCE PROCEDURE — STEP 4 COORDINATOR REVIEW
evidence.text: The coordinator reviews the appeal, conducts further investigation if necessary, and issues a written decision within 30 calendar days to the complainant, accused party, and cabinet member.

results.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
results.pass_condition: Policy requires each vendor system to fit the FERPA school-official exception: legitimate educational interest, institutional function, district direct control, and use/re-disclosure limits.

evidence.heading: CHILD ABUSE
evidence.text: The superintendent is authorized to conduct an internal investigation or take other necessary steps upon receiving information from a county department of social services or law enforcement that a suspected child abuse perpetrator is a district employee. Such information shall remain confidential except for district employees possessing a legitimate need for it. The superintendent shall notify the Colorado Department of Education of the investigation. Any school official who has reasonable cause to know or suspect a child has been subjected to abuse or neglect shall immediately report or cause a report to be made in accordance with District policies and state law.

results.revision: Each vendor system that accesses education records shall qualify under the FERPA school-official exception, with a legitimate educational interest, the District's direct control over records, and binding use and re-disclosure limits.

results.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
results.pass_condition: Policy binds vendors as 'school service contract providers' under the Colorado Student Data Transparency and Security Act (C.R.S. 22-16).

evidence.heading: LEGAL REFERENCES
evidence.text: The policy contains no explicit mention of automated decision systems, algorithms, AI, machine learning, predictive tools, or specific software/technology vendor requirements, beyond general references to "software purchases" as a category covered by standard District contract templates and the cross-reference to the Student Data Transparency and Security Act and Colorado Privacy Act (data protection statutes, not AI-specific provisions).

evidence.heading: NOTICE AND TRAINING
evidence.text: The district issues an annual written notice before each school year advising students, parents, employees, and the general public. Materials are provided in plain language and translated when possible. "Students and District employees will receive periodic training related to recognizing, reporting, and preventing discrimination and harassment."

evidence.heading: DISTRICT ACCOUNTABILITY COMMITTEE MEETINGS
evidence.text: "At least once each month during the school year"; open to the public with posted notices.

evidence.heading: PROHIBITED ACTIVITIES AND COMPLIANCE
evidence.text: Organizations must not engage in activities contrary to law, District policies/regulations or school rules; that disrupt or clearly threaten to disrupt the orderly operation of the District or school; or that would adversely affect the health, safety or welfare of any student or District employee. Noncompliance grounds revocation rights.

results.revision: Within thirty days after an adverse outcome materially influenced by a covered system, the District shall provide a plain-language description of the decision, the system's role, a simple process to request further information, and an explanation of the consumer's rights.

results.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.

evidence.heading: CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS
evidence.text: Coverage includes all student education records and personally identifiable information, plus personnel file information developed through District technology. Legal basis includes FERPA, the Colorado Open Records Act, and District Policies JRA/JRC and GBJ. Confidential data cannot be stored on a personally owned computer, PCD, or other electronic storage device, or on third-party systems, unless authorized by the executive director of information technology. Confidential information may be disclosed outside the District only as authorized by law and District policies, and within the District only to officials with a legitimate educational or supervisory interest.

evidence.heading: DISTRICT ACCOUNTABILITY COMMITTEE MEMBERSHIP
evidence.text: Sixteen voting members representing: students with disabilities, gifted/talented students, charter school students, English language learners, ethnic minority students, early childhood program students, elementary/middle/high school students, business/industry representatives, administrators, teachers, and classified employees. Additional non-voting ex officio members include a charter school-knowledgeable administrator (co-chair) and a Parent Engagement Liaison.

evidence.heading: SUPPORTIVE MEASURES
evidence.text: The district must offer accommodations and supportive measures "designed to protect the safety of all students and that preserve and restore equal access" to education. Students need not file formal complaints or wait for investigations to receive these supports. Additional accommodations are available for students with disabilities.

results.revision: The District shall retain, for at least three years after each consequential decision, records reasonably necessary to demonstrate compliance with this policy.

evidence.heading: KEY DEFINITIONS
evidence.text: Personally Identifiable Information includes data linked or linkable to a specific student enabling community identification, encompassing names, family member identifiers, addresses, social security numbers, student IDs, biometric records, and indirect identifiers like birth dates or maiden names.

evidence.heading: NOTIFICATION REQUIREMENTS
evidence.text: Each driver receives educational materials explaining 49 C.F.R. Part 382 requirements and District policy, and employee organization representatives are notified of the materials' availability. Required content includes: designated District persons to answer questions; categories of drivers subject to testing; information about safety-sensitive functions sufficient to clarify compliance periods; specific prohibited conduct; testing circumstances; testing procedures protecting the driver, testing integrity, and result validity; the requirement to submit to compliant tests; the definition of refusal and its consequences; violation consequences including immediate removal and referral/evaluation/treatment procedures; consequences for alcohol concentration of 0.02 or greater but less than 0.04; and information on drug/alcohol effects on health, work, and personal life, problem signs and symptoms, and intervention methods. Each driver signs a statement certifying receipt of these materials. The District informs drivers before testing if reasonably possible. For pre-employment tests, the District notifies the driver of results if requested within 60 calendar days of the employment application disposition notice. For positive random, reasonable-suspicion, or post-accident tests, the District notifies the driver and identifies the verified controlled substance(s). Upon employee request, the District forwards the duplicate specimen to a laboratory of the employee's choice, so long as it is Department of Health and Human Services certified.

results.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
results.pass_condition: Policy ensures records are sufficient to demonstrate compliance to the Attorney General.

evidence.heading: INTERNAL CONTROLS
evidence.text: The District maintains controls including: (1) written documentation of all transactions; (2) at least two authorized employees, as designated in this policy, signing off in advance on investments; and (3) periodic internal auditor review. The executive director shall ensure annual policy review with revisions submitted to the Board.

evidence.heading: PHYSICAL EDUCATION
evidence.text: Parents seeking to excuse children from physical education must submit written requests specifying: which curriculum portions the child cannot access; duration of inability; and reasons for inability. Principals may require documentation from licensed healthcare providers. Disagreements may be appealed to the appropriate assistant superintendent, with final appeal to the superintendent.

results.revision: Before deploying a covered system, the District shall obtain the developer's documentation of intended and known-harmful uses, training-data categories, known limitations, and instructions for appropriate use, monitoring, and human review.

evidence.text: Service animals must be "under the control of their handlers" via harness, leash, or tether, with exceptions where the handler has a disability preventing such use or control would interfere with task performance. On vehicles, animals must remain "near the individual they are accompanying and out of the aisle."

results.revision: Vendor contracts shall place education records under the District's direct control and limit their use to the contracted purpose, prohibiting reuse, resale, or re-disclosure.

evidence.heading: PROHIBITED TERMS (VOID UNLESS LEGAL COUNSEL PRE-APPROVES)
evidence.text: Any requirement that the District indemnify or hold harmless another person or entity. Any requirement that the District participate in binding arbitration or other extrajudicial process for dispute resolution. Any requirement that the District agree to limit liability of another person or entity for bodily injury, death, or damage to school district property. Any waiver, alteration, or limitation of the application of the Colorado Governmental Immunity Act. Any waiver, alteration, or limitation of the application of the Student Data Transparency and Security Act or the Colorado Privacy Act. Any conflict with Colorado law or associated rules under state statute.

evidence.heading: AUDIT SCOPE
evidence.text: Services focus on activities affecting compliance with accounting and financial standards, regulations, procedures and policies, including: reviewing financial information reliability, operating systems, and measurement/reporting mechanisms; examining compliance systems regarding governing laws and District policies; reviewing asset safeguarding means and verifying asset existence; recommending internal control improvements; reviewing external auditor performance and coordination; assisting in investigations of suspected law or policy violations; informing leadership of emerging internal audit trends; and reviewing employee compliance with significant financial policy requirements. Matters exclusively involving non-financial operations fall outside current scope.

results.revision: Before adopting a screener or predictive assessment, the District shall verify it meets the READ Act technical criteria, including validity, classification accuracy, and a public technical manual with research-based cutoff points.

evidence.heading: DEFINITIONS
evidence.text: Contract: an agreement between the District and one or more parties that creates mutual obligations that are enforceable or otherwise recognizable at law. Cooperative Purchasing Agreement: a process allowing the District to purchase goods/services at pre-negotiated rates using existing contracts awarded by other public agencies. District-Issued Contract: an agreement generated by the District for specific services/goods, requiring generation or minimum review/approval by Finance or legal counsel. Material Change: a modification to a standard District template or standard written terms and conditions that alters the legal obligations of a party. Service: work by non-District employees providing specialized knowledge/skills/expertise, typically intangible. Standard District Template: contracts reviewed/approved by legal counsel for construction, service agreements, software purchases, community partnerships, and other services. Standard Written Terms and Conditions: standard contract approved by legal counsel for goods purchases (excluding software). Vendor Contract: vendor-issued agreements requiring Finance or legal counsel review before execution.

results.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
results.pass_condition: Policy designates a trained human reviewer with explicit authority to approve, modify, or override a consequential decision materially influenced by a covered system, who does not default to the output.

evidence.heading: PERMISSIBLE INQUIRIES
evidence.text: District officials may ask only two questions: "(a) Is the animal required because of a disability? (b) What work or task has the animal been trained to perform?" Officials cannot inquire about the nature or extent of disability.

results.revision: A consequential decision materially influenced by a covered system shall be reviewed by a designated, trained District official who has authority to approve, modify, or override the decision and who does not default to the system's output.

results.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.

evidence.heading: ACCESS RIGHTS
evidence.text: Access Procedures: records must be examined in the presence of the principal or designee. Original records remain in District/school buildings. One copy is provided within a reasonable time at a cost not to exceed 25 cents per page. Test protocols and completed test instruments cannot be copied due to copyright/integrity concerns.

results.revision: The reviewer shall consider the relevant available primary evidence, be trained to conduct the review, and shall not default to the system's output.

evidence.heading: INITIAL CONFERENCE
evidence.text: Within two school days, administrators meet with complainants to discuss supportive measures, obtain clear complaint details, and explain resolution options (informal/formal), the district's obligation to prevent recurrence, available accommodations for students with disabilities, and confidentiality limitations.

results.revision: The reviewer shall have access to information sufficient to understand the system's intended use, material limitations, categories of inputs, and the principal factors used to generate its output.

results.pass_semantics: Built to the standard we expect the AG to set; subject to final rules.
results.pass_condition: Policy provides, on an adverse outcome, an opportunity for meaningful human review and reconsideration to the extent commercially reasonable.

evidence.heading: PROHIBITED CONDUCT
evidence.text: No driver shall have a prohibited alcohol concentration (0.02 or greater) in the body while on the job. No driver shall have any detectable quantity of a controlled substance in the body while on the job, except when authorized by a physician. No driver shall possess or consume alcohol, or possess or use a controlled substance (except as authorized by a physician), while on the job or on-call, on school premises or assigned sites, driving a District vehicle or personal vehicle on District business, or participating in school-sponsored or sanctioned activities. No driver shall consume alcohol during the four hours prior to driving. No driver shall consume alcohol during the eight hours following a motor vehicle accident. An exception applies to the controlled-substance prohibitions when a physician has advised that the substance does not adversely affect safe operation and has provided a written statement to that effect to the driver's supervisor.

results.revision: On an adverse outcome, the affected consumer shall have an opportunity for meaningful human review and reconsideration of the decision, to the extent commercially reasonable.

results.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.
results.pass_condition: Policy provides correction of factually inaccurate personal data (not opinions, predictions, scores, or protected evaluations).

evidence.heading: REQUIRED AUDIT REPORT CONTENTS
evidence.text: The audit report must include: (1) financial statements prepared in conformity with generally accepted governmental accounting principles, noted as district representations; (2) disclosures per the Financial Policies and Procedures Handbook, with supplemental schedules in State Board of Education format, aligned with audited statements; (3) coverage of all funds and activities of the school district; (4) a budget-to-actual comparison for each fund and activity; (5) the auditor's opinion on financial statements (including general fixed assets), with non-unqualified opinions requiring explanation; (6) disclosure of all instances of noncompliance with state law, including the Public School Finance Act of 1994, irrespective of materiality; and (7) a supplemental investment listing as of financial statement date.

gap: its focus on factually inaccurate data

results.revision: A consumer may request correction of factually incorrect or materially inaccurate personal data used in a decision; correction of opinions, predictions, scores, or protected evaluations is not required.

results.pass_semantics: Hard requirement — must fully pass; defensible today.

evidence.heading: PURPOSE AND GENERAL RETENTION STANDARD
evidence.text: "All District records except audio and video recordings, regardless of their location, shall be retained for the periods specified in the Colorado School District Records Management Manual prepared by the Colorado State Archives, as may be amended from time to time, unless a longer retention period is specified in state or federal law." This means the substantive category-by-category retention schedule for the great majority of District records (student records, personnel/employment records, financial and payroll records, contracts, correspondence, IT/network logs, minutes, etc.) is incorporated by reference to the external Colorado School District Records Management Manual rather than reproduced as a table inside the EHB policy text itself; where state or federal law prescribes a longer period than the Manual for a given record type, the longer period controls.

results.pass_semantics: Defensible reading taken where commenters/AG framing disagree; flagged for reconciliation.
